title: Coding problem - 1

content.problemStatement:
Let us start with a sorting problem.

Problem statementThere are two groups, one of NNN boys and the other of NNN girls numbered from 111 to NNN.

You are given two arrays AAA and BBB containing NNN numbers each, denoting the height of boys and girls in the group.
You have to form NNN couples, where each couple will consist of 111 boy and 111 girl.

Each couple has a **LIKENESS VALUE**, where


**LIKENESS VALUE = height of girl in the couple + height of boy in that couple.**
You have to form NNN couples in such a way that the maximum of **LIKENESS VALUE** of all the couples is minimum.

**Note:-  No boy or girl can form more than one couple.**


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN:- number of boys and number of girls in the group.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers, denoting the height of NNN boys.
The third line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers, denoting the height of NNN girls.


Output Format

For each test case, print the maximum LIKENESS VALUE in a new line.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains an integer N, the number of boys and girls. The second line contains N space‑separated integers denoting the heights of the boys. The third line contains N space‑separated integers denoting the heights of the girls.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the minimum possible value of the maximum LIKENESS VALUE among all N couples.

solution.approach: Sort the boys in non‑decreasing order and the girls in non‑decreasing order. Pair the smallest boy with the tallest girl, the second smallest boy with the second tallest girl, and so on. For each pair compute the sum (likeness value) and keep the maximum of these sums. This maximum is the smallest possible maximum likeness because any other pairing would increase at least one of these sums (rearrangement inequality). The algorithm runs in O(N log N) per test case due to sorting, and uses O(N) additional space for the arrays.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case (sorting dominates)
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) auxiliary space for the two arrays
